info.title: Workday REST API
info.description:
The Workday REST API provides programmatic access to Workday Human Capital Management (HCM), Financial Management, Payroll, and other enterprise data.

Key capabilities include:
- Human Resources management (workers, organizations, positions)
- Staffing operations (hiring, job changes, terminations)
- Absence and time-off management
- Compensation and benefits administration
- Payroll and pay group management
- Recruiting and talent management
- Financial management (cost centers, companies, ledger accounts)
- Custom report execution via Report-as-a-Service (RaaS)

All REST API endpoints follow the pattern `/ccx/api/{service}/{version}/{tenant}/{resource}`. Authentication is performed via OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens obtained from the tenant-specific token endpoint.

Pagination is supported through `limit` and `offset` query parameters. The default page size is 20 records with a maximum of 100.

baseUrl.description: The Workday data center hostname. Common values include wd2-impl-services1.workday.com (US Implementation), wd3-impl-services1.workday.com (EU Implementation), wd5-impl-services1.workday.com (US Production), or your custom vanity URL.

tags.name: Workers
tags.description: Worker lifecycle management: retrieve, search, and manage worker records

tags.name: Organizations
tags.description: Supervisory organizations, cost centers, companies, and organizational hierarchies

tags.name: Staffing
tags.description: Staffing transactions: hiring, job changes, terminations, and position management

tags.name: Absence Management
tags.description: Time-off requests, absence entries, leave of absence, and accrual balances

tags.name: Compensation
tags.description: Compensation plans, grades, packages, and compensation change history

tags.name: Benefits
tags.description: Benefits enrollment, plans, and coverage management

tags.name: Payroll
tags.description: Pay groups, payroll inputs, payslips, and payment elections

tags.name: Recruiting
tags.description: Job requisitions, candidates, applications, and offer management

tags.name: Common
tags.description: Common API discovery and metadata endpoints

get.description: Returns a list of available REST API services for the tenant. Use this to discover which services and versions are available.

Returns a paginated list of workers in the tenant. Workers include both employees and contingent workers. Use query parameters to filter and limit results. Results include personal data, employment data, and organizational assignments based on security permissions.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search string to filter workers by name or worker ID.

get.description: Returns detailed information for a specific worker by their Workday ID. Includes personal data, employment data, compensation, organization assignments, and role data based on security permissions.
Returns the business contact information for a worker, including work email addresses, work phone numbers, and work addresses.
Returns personal information for a worker, including legal name, preferred name, date of birth, gender, marital status, and nationality.
Returns workers with their staffing-related information including positions, job profiles, supervisory organizations, and employment details.

200.description: Paginated list of workers with staffing data

get.summary: Get staffing details for a worker
get.description: Returns detailed staffing information for a specific worker, including current position, job profile, supervisory organization, location, and employment status.

post.summary: Initiate a job change for a worker
post.description: Creates a job change event for the specified worker. This can include changes to position, job profile, location, supervisory organization, compensation, and other staffing attributes.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of job profiles defined in the tenant. Job profiles define the job classification, job family, management level, and other attributes of a position.
Returns a paginated list of positions within the tenant, including filled and unfilled positions. Positions define the organizational slot that a worker fills.
Returns a paginated list of supervisory organizations. A supervisory organization defines the reporting structure and is associated with a manager, positions, and workers.
Returns detailed information for a specific supervisory organization, including its manager, subordinate organizations, assigned positions, and workers.

parameters.name: supervisoryOrgId
parameters.description: The Workday ID of the supervisory organization.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of organizations across all types including supervisory, cost center, company, region, custom, and matrix organizations.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter organizations by type (e.g., Supervisory, Cost_Center, Company, Region, Custom).

get.description: Returns a paginated list of cost center organizations. Cost centers represent financial groupings used for budgeting and expense tracking.
Returns a paginated list of company organizations. Companies represent the legal entities within the Workday tenant.
Returns a paginated list of work locations defined in the tenant. Locations represent physical work sites and include address information.
Returns detailed information for a specific work location including address, time zone, and location hierarchy data.

parameters.name: locationId
parameters.description: The Workday ID of the location.

post.summary: Request time off for a worker
post.description: Creates a new time-off request for the specified worker. The request must specify the time-off type, dates, and optionally the quantity of hours or days.

get.summary: Get time-off entries for a worker
get.description: Returns time-off entries for the specified worker, including approved, pending, and denied requests. Results include the time-off type, dates, status, and quantity.

parameters.name: fromDate
parameters.description: Filter entries starting from this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: toDate
parameters.description: Filter entries up to this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

get.summary: Get time-off plan balances for a worker
get.description: Returns current time-off plan balances for the specified worker. Includes accrued, used, and remaining balances for each enrolled time-off plan.

get.summary: Get leave of absence records for a worker
get.description: Returns leave of absence records for the specified worker, including type, start and end dates, status, and reason.

200.description: List of leave of absence records

get.summary: Get compensation plans for a worker
get.description: Returns the current compensation plans assigned to a worker, including base pay, allowances, bonuses, and other compensation elements.

get.summary: Get compensation history for a worker
get.description: Returns the compensation change history for a worker, including effective dates, change reasons, and before/after values.
Returns a list of compensation grades defined in the tenant. Compensation grades define salary ranges and are associated with job profiles.
Returns compensation packages defined in the tenant. A compensation package groups together compensation plans that are commonly assigned together.

get.summary: Get benefit elections for a worker
get.description: Returns the current benefit elections for a worker, including health insurance, dental, vision, life insurance, retirement, and other benefit plan enrollments.
Returns a list of benefit plans available in the tenant, including health, dental, vision, life insurance, retirement, and other benefit plan types.

get.summary: Get pay slips for a worker
get.description: Returns pay slips for the specified worker. Each pay slip includes gross pay, net pay, deductions, taxes, and payment details.

parameters.name: fromDate
parameters.description: Filter pay slips starting from this pay period date (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: toDate
parameters.description: Filter pay slips up to this pay period date (YYYY-MM-DD).

get.summary: Get payment elections for a worker
get.description: Returns the payment election (direct deposit) configuration for a worker, including bank account details, routing numbers, and distribution percentages.
Returns a paginated list of pay groups defined in the tenant. Pay groups define the pay schedule and payroll processing configuration.
Returns a paginated list of job requisitions. Each requisition includes the job posting details, hiring manager, position, and current status.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by requisition status (e.g., Open, Filled, Closed, Frozen).

get.description: Returns detailed information for a specific job requisition, including position details, hiring team, requirements, and application status.

parameters.name: jobRequisitionId
parameters.description: The Workday ID of the job requisition.

get.summary: Get candidates for a job requisition
get.description: Returns a list of candidates who have applied for a specific job requisition, including their application status and stage.

parameters.name: jobRequisitionId
parameters.description: The Workday ID of the job requisition.

get.description: Executes a Workday custom report via Report-as-a-Service (RaaS) and returns the results. The report must be configured as a web service in Workday. Results can be returned as JSON or CSV.

parameters.name: reportOwner
parameters.description: The owner of the custom report (usually a username or ISU name).

parameters.name: reportName
parameters.description: The name of the custom report as configured in Workday.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Response format for the report output.

workday_oauth2.description:
Workday uses OAuth 2.0 for API authentication. Register an API Client in Workday via the 'Register API Client for Integrations' task to obtain client credentials. The tenant-specific token endpoint is used to exchange credentials for access tokens.

Supported grant types:
- Authorization Code: For interactive applications
- Authorization Code with PKCE: Recommended for CORS-enabled cross-origin requests
- JWT Bearer: For server-to-server integrations using X.509 certificates
- SAML Bearer: For applications using SAML SSO
- Refresh Token: For exchanging refresh tokens for new access tokens

Access tokens expire after 60 minutes by default. Use refresh tokens for long-running integrations.

workday_api_key.description: Workday API key authentication using a bearer token passed in the Authorization header. Obtain the token by first authenticating via OAuth 2.0 and then using the resulting access token as a bearer token in subsequent API requests.
workday_api_key.name: Authorization

workday_basic.description: HTTP Basic Authentication using an Integration System User (ISU) username and password. This method is supported for backward compatibility but OAuth 2.0 is recommended for new integrations.

TenantParam.name: tenant
TenantParam.description: The Workday tenant name (e.g., 'mycompany' or 'mycompany_impl1' for implementation tenants).

WorkerIdParam.name: workerId
WorkerIdParam.description: The Workday ID of the worker.

LimitParam.name: limit
LimitParam.description: Maximum number of results to return per page. Default is 20, maximum is 100.

OffsetParam.name: offset
OffsetParam.description: Number of results to skip for pagination. Use with limit to page through results.

BadRequest.description: Bad request. The request was malformed or contained invalid parameters.

Unauthorized.description: Authentication failed. The access token is missing, expired, or invalid.

Forbidden.description: Authorization failed. The authenticated user does not have the required security permissions for this resource.

NotFound.description: The requested resource was not found.

field.description: The field that caused the error, if applicable.

InstanceReference.description: A reference to a Workday object instance.

id.description: The Workday ID of the referenced object.

descriptor.description: A human-readable display name for the referenced object.

href.description: The API URL of the referenced object.

id.description: The Workday ID of the worker.

href.description: API URL for this worker resource.

workerID.description: The employee or contingent worker ID.

originalHireDate.description: Original hire date, which may differ from hire date for rehired workers.

continuousServiceDate.description: Continuous service date used for benefits and seniority calculations.

terminationDate.description: Termination date, if the worker has been terminated.

jobTitle.description: Business title for the worker's primary job.

startDate.description: Start date of the position assignment.

effectiveDate.description: Effective date of the job change.

dailyQuantity.description: Hours or days of time off for this date.

comment.description: Optional comment for this time-off day.

scheduled.description: Amount approved but not yet taken.

Report_Entry.description: Array of report row objects. Field names match the report column aliases configured in Workday.
